summary:
Build a trie (also called a prefix tree), a data structure designed for storing and looking up strings by their character sequences. It is commonly used for fast prefix-based queries such as autocomplete and spell checking.

Implement a Trie class with three methods: insert(word) adds a word to the structure, search(word) returns true only if that exact word was inserted earlier, and startsWith(prefix) returns true if any stored word begins with the given prefix.

examples.output: [null, null, true, false, true, null, true]
examples.explanation: After inserting "apple", search("apple") is true but search("app") is false because "app" was never inserted as a full word. startsWith("app") is true since "apple" begins with it. After inserting "app", search("app") becomes true.

examples.output: [null, null, null, true, false, true, false]
examples.explanation: search("do") is false because only the complete word "dog" was stored, but startsWith("do") is true. startsWith("dox") is false since no stored word begins with that prefix.

constraints: word and prefix consist of lowercase English letters
At most 30000 calls in total will be made to insert, search, and startsWith
Constructor calls return null; insert returns null
